_comment: Brent crude (Europe Brent Spot Price FOB) converted to GBP. USD series sourced from EIA daily table at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/rbrted.htm (US federal public-domain). GBP/USD spot is the BoE 4pm daily fix (XUDLUSS). Refreshed weekly by the editorial pipeline. 2026-07-13 refresh: latest EIA Europe Brent Spot print is 2026-07-06 = $69.56/bbl (verified via the FRED mirror DCOILBRENTEU, which republishes the EIA series; EIA publishes the dated series with a few days' lag, so this trails the front-month futures quotes carried in news media), divided by the BoE XUDLUSS 4pm fix for 6 Jul 2026 = 1.3356 (from our own ingested gbp_usd series, relay-fed), giving 69.56 / 1.3356 = 52.0815 -> 52.08 GBP/bbl. The triplet round-trips: 52.08 x 1.3356 = 69.56. This mirrors exactly the adapter derivation in adapters/eiaBrent.ts (latest EIA period / nearest fix, pairing skew 0 days). Context for the level: Brent has continued unwinding the April 2026 conflict spike (peak $138.21 on 2026-04-07) into the high-60s USD, with a brief early-July rebound on renewed US-Iran tensions.
